samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. and the inputs are two 8-bit operands. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. The design supports both signed and unsigned division operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst">The reset signal to initialize or reset the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit the output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">Idle state.</state>
        <state id="DIVIDE">Divide state.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="dividend_reg" width="8">Stores the dividend.</reg>
        <reg id="divisor_reg" width="8">Stores the divisor.</reg>
        <reg id="remainder_reg" width="9">Stores the remainder.</reg>
        <reg id="quotient_reg" width="8">Stores the quotient.</reg>
        <reg id="start_cnt" width="1">Indicates if the division process starts.</reg>
        <reg id="cnt" width="4">Counts the number of iterations.</reg>
        <reg id="neg_divisor_reg" width="9">Stores the negative divisor.</reg>
        <reg id="sr_reg" width="17">Shift register for storing the dividend and remainder.</reg>
        <reg id="mux_out_reg" width="9">Multiplexer output.</reg>
        <reg id="carry_out_reg" width="1">Carry-out flag.</reg>
        <reg id="res_valid_reg" width="1">Indicates if the result is valid.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Operation Start
        always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
            if (rst) begin
                dividend_reg <= 8'b0;
                divisor_reg <= 8'b0;
                remainder_reg <= 9'b0;
                quotient_reg <= 8'b0;
                start_cnt <= 1'b0;
                cnt <= 4'b0;
                neg_divisor_reg <= 9'b0;
                sr_reg <= 17'b0;
                mux_out_reg <= 9'b0;
                carry_out_reg <= 1'b0;
                res_valid_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                if (opn_valid && !res_valid_reg) begin
                    dividend_reg <= dividend;
                    divisor_reg <= divisor;
                    remainder_reg <= {1'b0, dividend} << 1; // Shift dividend left by 1 bit
                    neg_divisor_reg <= ~{1'b0, divisor} + 1; // Negate divisor
                    start_cnt <= 1'b1;
                    cnt <= 4'b1;
                end
                
                // Division Process
                if (start_cnt) begin
                    if (cnt[3]) begin
                        // Division Complete
                        start_cnt <= 1'b0;
                        sr_reg <= {remainder_reg, quotient_reg};
                        res_valid_reg <= 1'b1;
                    end else begin
                        // Perform Subtraction
                        {carry_out_reg, mux_out_reg} <= sr_reg + neg_divisor_reg;
                        
                        // Update Shift Register
                        sr_reg <= {sr_reg[16], sr_reg[16:1]} | {carry_out_reg, sr_reg[0]};
                        cnt <= cnt + 1;
                    end
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Result Validity
        always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
            if (rst) begin
                res_valid_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                if (cnt == 4'b1000) begin
                    res_valid_reg <= 1'b1;
                end else if (res_valid_reg && res_ready) begin
                    res_valid_reg <= 1'b0;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
